CREATE TABLE test_table (col1 VARCHAR(50), col2 VARCHAR(50));
SELECT "<>"(col1, col2) AS result FROM test_table;
SELECT col1, "<>"(col1, col2) AS not_equal FROM test_table WHERE "<>"(col1, 'ab');
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(50));
SELECT a.col1, b.name FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON "<>"(a.col1, b.name);
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE "<>"(col1, 'value')) AS not_equal_count;
SELECT col1 FROM test_table WHERE "<>"(col1, col2) AND "<>"(col2, 'aa');
CREATE TABLE join_table (id INT, status VARCHAR(10));
SELECT t1.col1, jt.status FROM test_table t1 INNER JOIN join_table jt ON "<>"(t1.col2, jt.status);
SELECT col1 FROM test_table WHERE "<>"(col1, 'abc') OR "<>"(col2, 'xyz');
SELECT "<>"(col1, col2) AS comparison FROM (SELECT col1, col2 FROM test_table WHERE id = 1) AS subquery;
